title: Pro Rigging Slide Course
description: Advanced classroom slides — vertical plate clamps, spreader bars, lifting beams, and non-symmetric sling tension. For experienced riggers building on competency fundamentals.

slides.title: Pro rigging slide course
slides.summary: Advanced below-the-hook rigging for experienced riggers.
bullets: Builds on rigger competency — clamps, spreader bars, lifting beams, asymmetric tension.
Classroom delivery: clicker, TV cast, phone, offline save.
Not a substitute for manufacturer data, engineered lifts, or qualified supervision.

slides.title: When this course applies
slides.summary: Pro rigging goes beyond basic sling-and-shackle picks.
bullets: Plate handling with vertical clamps
Multi-leg bridles with spreader bars or lifting beams
Loads where leg tensions are not equal (COG offset, unequal leg length)
Critical or engineered lifts requiring documented planning

slides.title: Competency baseline
slides.summary: Prerequisites before pro topics.
bullets: Qualified / competent worker per OHSR and site policy
WLL, design factor, hitch types, and pre-use inspection fluency
Sling tension basics and angle factors from competency course
Stop work when ratings, geometry, or planning are uncertain

slides.title: Plate clamp purpose
slides.summary: Clamps grip steel plate edges for vertical or horizontal lifts.
bullets: Transfer load through clamp jaws — not through sling friction alone
Rated for plate thickness range and hardness
Marked WLL for vertical lift unless rated for other orientations
Never substitute a clamp for a hook on unknown material

slides.title: Jaw type and grip
slides.summary: Friction and tooth pattern must match plate condition.
bullets: Smooth jaws for painted or stainless plate (higher clamping force)
Serrated jaws for mill scale and rough carbon steel
Inspect jaw wear, springs, and locking pins before each use
Grease, paint, or ice on plate reduces grip — clean or reject

slides.title: Vertical pick with one clamp
slides.summary: Single-clamp picks need extra controls.
bullets: Load can swing or peel if COG is off-centre or plate flexes
Use tag line; keep body out of fall zone
Often requires two clamps with a spreader or equalized bridle
Follow manufacturer chart for min/max plate thickness

slides.title: Twin clamp rigging
slides.summary: Two clamps share load when geometry is controlled.
bullets: Equal leg length from hook to each clamp
Spreader bar or rigid link prevents clamps from closing toward each other
Verify combined WLL and equalization — uneven grip overloads one clamp
Use safety latches on hooks; prevent sideload on clamp shackle

slides.title: Horizontal plate clamps
slides.summary: Horizontal models differ from vertical — read the nameplate.
bullets: Locking mechanism must be engaged per manufacturer procedure
Not interchangeable with vertical clamps unless dual-rated
Flipping plate during lift can release grip — plan path and stops
Remove from service if teeth are worn below manufacturer limit
slides.ohrsRef: OHSR 15.39 — protect slings from sharp plate edges

slides.title: Why spreader bars
slides.summary: Spreaders change sling geometry and protect the load.
bullets: Keep sling legs nearly vertical — reduce horizontal compression on load
Increase stability for wide or flexible loads (duct, vessel sections)
Transfer compression through the bar; slings carry tension only
Bar must be rated for compression and connection points

slides.title: Spreader vs lifting beam
slides.summary: Know which device you are using.
bullets: Spreader bar: slings go up to hook, bar spreads legs down to load
Lifting beam: beam hangs from hook; load hangs directly from beam
Spreader reduces top sling angle; beam supports load at multiple points
Ratings and inspection requirements differ — read nameplate

slides.title: Spreader bar angles
slides.summary: Top sling angle still affects tension.
bullets: Longer spreader → lower top sling angle → higher top leg tension
Check WLL at actual span and angle, not just vertical pick
Pad contact points to avoid point loading on bar ends
Tag lines control rotation — spreader can roll if COG is high

slides.title: Adjustable spreaders
slides.summary: Telescoping or pin-adjusted spreaders need position locks.
bullets: Pin holes and locks must be fully engaged before lift
Re-rate or consult engineer when span differs from chart
Mark configured span on lift plan
Inspect welds, end fittings, and lugs each shift

slides.title: Spreader failure modes
slides.summary: Common issues in the field.
bullets: Buckling if bar is not designed for compression at that span
Unequal leg length → unequal load at pick points → twist or slip
Side pull on bar lugs — keep hook over centre of spread
Mixed hardware ratings in the assembly

slides.title: Lifting beam basics
slides.summary: Beam carries bending moment; slings support the beam.
bullets: Top rigging: bridle to crane hook (often 2-leg at angle)
Bottom rigging: load hung from multiple bottom lugs
Beam must be rated for load plus self-weight and moment
Engineered beams may require proof test documentation

slides.title: Bending and lug loading
slides.summary: Not all lugs share load equally without analysis.
bullets: Simply supported beam: centre lug sees highest moment
Multiple lugs reduce moment when load is distributed
Off-centre COG increases load on one lug
Use engineered drawing or calc for non-uniform picks

slides.title: Top bridle on beams
slides.summary: Top sling angles add tension beyond load weight.
bullets: Short top bridle → high angle → high leg tension
Check beam top lugs for combined vertical and lateral load
Swivel hooks reduce twist but do not fix overload
Balance beam before ground rigging when possible

slides.title: Adjustable counterweights
slides.summary: Some beams use movable ballast — follow procedure.
bullets: Counterweight position changes COG of beam assembly
Document position on lift plan; verify with engineer
Never modify ballast without authorization
Include beam self-weight in crane load chart selection

slides.title: Inspection points
slides.summary: Below-the-hook devices per B30.20.
bullets: Welds, cracks, deformation, lug wear
Load test date and serial / ID marking
Shackle and pin fit; no homemade repairs
Remove from service if rating plate missing or illegible

slides.title: Equal legs ≠ equal tension
slides.summary: Geometry and COG split the load.
bullets: Offset COG shifts more weight to shorter or steeper legs
Unequal leg length changes tension even at same angle to horizontal
Basket and choker hits change share when load tilts
Never assume 50/50 on a two-leg unless geometry proves it

slides.title: COG and pick points
slides.summary: Locate COG before selecting rigging.
bullets: Trial lift low: observe which legs load up
Shift pick points or add counterweight only when planned
Tag line without changing COG
Document as asymmetric on lift plan

slides.title: Calculating unequal shares
slides.summary: Use trig or rigging software for non-trivial picks.
bullets: Resolve forces at pick point with known leg angles
Worst leg must stay under sling WLL at hitch type
Include dynamic factor if procedure requires
When in doubt, engineer or manufacturer review

slides.title: Three- and four-leg bridles
slides.summary: Multi-leg does not mean equal load.
bullets: Legs on short side of COG carry more
Idle leg on three-leg bridle — never count three at 100%
Four-leg: typically plan for three legs sharing (one may be slack)
Adjust with turnbuckles only when rated and procedure allows

slides.title: Field verification
slides.summary: Prove the rig before full height.
bullets: Initial inching: watch all legs, listen for slack shock
Measure deflection or use load pins when specified
Stop if one leg maxes while others are slack
Re-rig rather than forcing crane or winch

slides.title: Software and charts
slides.summary: Tools support but do not replace judgment.
bullets: Rigging calc apps need accurate weight, COG, and angles
Cross-check with manual calc for critical lifts
Keep printout with lift plan
Update calc if rigging or load changes on site

slides.title: Combining devices
slides.summary: Real picks mix clamps, spreaders, and beams.
bullets: Draw sketch: hook → top rigging → device → bottom rigging → load
Lowest WLL in chain governs assembly
Account for hardware weight in crane chart
Brief entire crew on roles and hand signals

slides.title: Critical lift checklist
slides.summary: Document when triggers are met.
bullets: Written plan, competent person, exclusion zones
Rated capacity verified for every component
Weather, ground, and crane configuration reviewed
Stop work authority understood by all

slides.title: Practice test
slides.summary: Reinforce pro rigging topics.
bullets: Vertical plate clamps and grip limits
Spreader bars vs lifting beams
Non-symmetric sling tension and COG
Take the pro rigging practice test on pull

slides.title: Course close
slides.summary: Pro rigging demands proof, not assumptions.
bullets: Manufacturer data and standards govern over memory
Competency course + lessons for fundamentals
Questions? Stop the lift and escalate
Thank you — work safe.
